choice: Yes — bind contributions and deferrals to the salary stream that funds them. While a person's salary stream is active in a given year, deferrals/match accrue; the year the stream ends, they stop automatically, with no separate trigger. Contributions are a function of active earned income, so they should derive from the same stream rather than carry their own lifetime.
rationale: Income stopping is already handled by the stream ending (now settled). But contributions/deferrals are derived savings behaviors, not income — if they're configured independently they could keep firing after earned income is gone, which is wrong. Binding them to the funding stream's active window is what makes the income-stream model complete on the outflow/savings side.
